title: salix × subglabra
intro: Salix × subglabra, also known as the hybrid willow, is a cross between Salix fragilis and Salix alba, resulting in a plant with unique characteristics and medicinal properties. The leaves, bark, and roots of Salix × subglabra are used in herbal medicine, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling. The plant has been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of Salix × subglabra can be made into infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used internally and externally to promote healing and well-being.
